description: Sony IMX296 1.58MP global-shutter, 1-lane MIPI, color BGGR Bayer. Generic camera profile for q6a_camstream (all sensor-specific config lives here; the scripts are camera-agnostic).

color_defaults._comment: Fixed WB fallbacks (a grey-card --calibrate overrides them). 1.60/1.52 under-corrected (residual green, and blue is the more deficient channel); nudged to 1.80/1.95 to neutralise under indoor light. Fixed WB is stable (AWB drifts magenta on non-gray rooms); a small sensor thermal-warmup drift remains.

ae._comment: Sensor auto-exposure: median-metered, exposure-only (fixed VMAX). target = raw high-byte median.

awb._comment: Auto white balance gain clamp (damped gray-world). Green-dominant Bayer -> R/B gains > 1; range covers tungsten..daylight for this sensor.

tonemap._comment: Display tone curve. gamma<1 lifts shadows; target_mean is the digital brightness the tone-map aims the frame at. Applied on the GPU (#define GAMMA) and the CPU-fallback LUT from one source.

shadow_tint._comment: Shadow green compensation (sensor low-light characteristic): R,B fall off faster than G at low signal, so shadows go green even at correct WB. In dark output pixels, pull R,B up toward G (only when below it -> reduces green, never magenta), tapering to 0 by 'knee' (output-green 0..255). r/b = fraction of the G-gap closed in deep shadow.

shading._comment: Post-processing for the calibrated radial shade map (imx296_wb.npz -> 'shade'). This fisheye has heavy corner vignetting + colour shading; the grey-card fit over-corrects the dark noisy corners into blue/magenta 'trash'. Smooth the grid to kill per-cell spikes, keep 85% of the colour correction, and clamp both the colour ratio and the vignetting gain so no corner cell over-tints or over-amplifies. Omit this section for a generic pass-through.

ccm._comment: Lab-calibrated color-correction matrices from Raspberry Pi's IMX296 libcamera tuning (rpi.ccm, src/ipa/rpi/vc4/data/imx296.json). Interpolated to `ct` at load. Rows sum to ~1 (neutral-preserving).
